title: vaccinium myrtillus subsp. oreophilum
intro: Vaccinium myrtillus subsp. oreophilum, also known as the mountain bilberry, is a subspecies of the bilberry plant, belonging to the heath family. This plant is rich in bioactive compounds, including anthocyanins, which have been found to have medicinal properties, such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The mountain bilberry has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and cardiovascular diseases, due to its potential health benefits in reducing inflammation, improving blood circulation, and protecting against cell damage. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, capsules, or extracts, which are used in traditional medicine and are also available as dietary supplements.
